steps.title: Restate the criterion visually
steps.content: We need data points where the predicted $y$ (the line) is greater than the actual $y$ (the dot). On the scatter plot, those are the points that sit below the line of best fit.

steps.title: Count the dots that lie below the line
steps.content: Working left to right along the line of best fit, the dots that fall clearly below it are at approximately $x \approx 2.5$, $3.5$, $4$, $5$, $7.5$, and $9$. That is 6 points whose predicted value is greater than the actual value. The answer is 6.
